senses.gloss: to be in good or perfect condition
to be honest, to be upright
to suit, respond, answer the purpose, be adequate

examples.text:
11 Century CE, Wallada bint al-Mustakfi, أنا واللّه أصلح للمعالي
أَنَا وَ الْلَّهِ أَصْلُحُ لِلْمَعَالِي ، وَأَمْشِي مِشْيَتِي وَأَتِيهُ تِيهَا
ʔanā wa l-llahi ʔaṣluḥu lilmaʕālī , waʔamšī mišyatī waʔatīhu tīhā
I am, by Allah, adequate for high positions. And am going my way, with pride.

senses.gloss: to be in good or perfect condition
to be honest, to be upright

examples.text: وَإِنِ ٱمْرَأَةٌ خَافَتْ مِن بَعْلِهَا نُشُوزًا أَوْ إِعْرَاضًا فَلَا جُنَاحَ عَلَيْهِمَا أَن يُصْلِحَا بَيْنَهُمَا صُلْحًا وَٱلصُّلْحُ خَيْرٌ
examples.english: And if a woman fears from her husband any inharmony or rebuffing, there is no blame on them if they reconcile. Indeed, reconciliation is good!

etymology.text: From صَلَحَ (ṣalaḥa, “to be right; to be adequate; to be sound, to be whole etc.”). Possibly influenced by Aramaic סָלַח (sālaḥ, “to forgive”).
